name: A167 DURHAM WESTERN BY PASS 76

surface_od_m.qualifier: as written: Ground level (m O D) 94.65

units: metres below ground level; typed BGS/Wimpey Geotech site-investigation log, transcribed verbatim

annotations: Reduced Level cross-check: base at 94.15 m O D (94.65 - 0.50) — consistent
boring commenced 28/3/90

intervals.verbatim: Firm to stiff mottled orange brown and grey sandy silty CLAY
annotations: Reduced Level cross-check: base at 92.15 m O D (94.65 - 2.50) — consistent
water: water added to assist boring from ground level to 2.50m (remarks) and may have masked groundwater

intervals.verbatim: Firm to stiff brown sandy silty CLAY with some fine to coarse angular to subrounded gravel and occasional lenses of grey silty clay. Faintly laminated towards base
annotations: Reduced Level cross-check: base at 88.15 m O D (94.65 - 6.50) — consistent
water: DRY / DRY recorded in water-depth column at 3.00-3.10m
date advanced to 29/3 at 3.10m

intervals.verbatim: Brown laminated silty CLAY with many dustings of pale brown silt. Many subhorizontal polished fissures
annotations: Reduced Level cross-check: base at 87.15 m O D (94.65 - 7.50) — consistent

intervals.verbatim: Medium dense pale yellowish brown uniform fine SAND with occasional fine angular coal gravel and many bands of dark greyish brown silty fine sand and occasional bands of brownish-grey silty clay
annotations: coal present only as detrital 'fine angular coal gravel' within glaciofluvial sand — not a coal seam
unit continues across sheets 1-2 ('See previous sheet'); base boundary at 15.50m on sheet 2, 79.15 m O D (94.65 - 15.50) — consistent

intervals.verbatim: Medium dense pale brown laminated very sandy SILT with many thin bands of yellowish brown fine sand containing occasional fine angular coal gravel
annotations: laminated very sandy SILT (soil, not siltstone) — glaciolacustrine drift
detrital coal gravel only, not a seam
Reduced Level cross-check: base at 75.15 m O D (94.65 - 19.50) — consistent
water: DRY / DRY recorded in water-depth column at 19.50m; sheet notes 'See next sheet'; date 30/3

intervals.verbatim: Medium dense pale yellowish brown uniform fine SAND with occasional laminae of brown silt, occasional bands of dark brownish grey clayey silty fine sand and occasional fine angular coal gravel
annotations: continues on sheet 3; base boundary at 24.00m, 70.65 m O D (94.65 - 24.00) — consistent
detrital coal gravel only, not a seam
water: water added to assist boring 19.50m to 30.00m (remarks) and may have masked groundwater

intervals.verbatim: Medium dense pale brown and yellowish brown laminated very sandy SILT with occasional coarse sand and fine angular gravel sized coal fragments and many bands of yellowish brown fine sand
annotations: laminated very sandy SILT (soil) — glaciolacustrine drift
detrital coal fragments only, not a seam
Reduced Level cross-check: base at 68.65 m O D (94.65 - 26.00) — consistent

intervals.verbatim: Medium dense pale yellowish brown fine SAND with occasional thin laminae of brown silty clay and occasional coarse sand and fine angular gravel sized fragments of coal
annotations: detrital coal fragments only, not a seam
END OF BOREHOLE at 30.00m
water: DRY recorded in water-depth column at 30.00m

piezometer installed at 29.00m (piezometer symbol on log); backfill 30.00-28.00m gravel, 28.00-10.00m sand spoil, 10.00-1.50m 1:1 bentonite/cement grout, 1.50m-ground level concrete; stopcock cover concreted over installation

notes: Modern typed Wimpey Geotech / BGS site-investigation borehole record (Fig. 83), 3 sheets, legible throughout. Boring method Cable Percussion; equipment Pilcon Wayfarer; boring 200mm to 19.50m / 150mm to 30.00m; casing 200mm to 15.00m / 150mm to 26.00m; orientation vertical. Commenced 28/3/90; originator DJM/AMcL 30/3/90 & 2/4/90; checked/approved AMcL 3/7/90. Handwritten archive reference 'NZ24SE/231' at head of each sheet (BGS index number, not a seam correlation). Entire 30m sequence is superficial (Quaternary glacial) drift — firm/stiff sandy silty CLAYs over interbedded medium-dense glaciofluvial SANDs and laminated glaciolacustrine very sandy SILTs; no bedrock ('post'/'metal') and no coal seams reached. 'Coal' appears throughout only as detrital fine angular coal gravel/fragments within the drift, so all 9 intervals classed drift and no seam_marks recorded. OD levels in the log's O D column cross-check exactly against ground level 94.65 at every boundary; no clerk arithmetic slips found. Remarks: water was added to assist boring from ground level to 2.50m and from 19.50m to 30.00m and may have masked the occurrence of ground-water (hole logged DRY in the water-depth column at 3.00-3.10m, 19.50m and 30.00m). Final depth 30.00m matches index depth.
